pm.problem: HR teams at SMBs (50-500 employees) spend 40% of their time on manual tasks: payroll, leave tracking, onboarding paperwork. Existing tools (BambooHR, Workday) are too expensive or too complex.
pm.solution: An AI-first HRMS that automates payroll, leave management, and onboarding via conversational workflows. Employees interact with a Slack/WhatsApp bot; managers get a dashboard with predictive attrition alerts.
pm.target_user: HR Managers at SMBs (India/SEA focus), 50–500 employees
mvp_features: Conversational leave request & approval via WhatsApp bot
Automated payroll with tax compliance (India GST/TDS)
AI onboarding wizard that generates role-specific task lists
pm.go_to_market: Bottom-up: free tier for <20 employees, paid at ₹499/employee/month
pm.tagline: Your AI-powered HR team — without the HR headcount.

design_system.style: Clean SaaS, light mode default with dark mode toggle

screens.name: Dashboard
components: KPI cards (headcount, attrition risk, pending approvals)

screens.name: Employee Directory
components: Card grid with avatar, role, risk badge

key_user_flows: Employee requests leave via WhatsApp → manager notified → approves in dashboard
HR runs payroll: selects month → reviews → confirms → payslips auto-sent

tech_choices.ml: scikit-learn attrition model served via FastAPI

marketing.positioning: The only HRMS built for WhatsApp-first teams in India & SEA
marketing.icp: HR Manager, 28-40yo, SMB in India/SEA, uses WhatsApp for everything, frustrated with Excel/BambooHR
channels: SEO: 'HR software India SMB', 'payroll automation India'
marketing.launch_hook: The HR manager who replaced 3 spreadsheets with one WhatsApp bot

investor_concerns: WhatsApp Business API approval is notoriously slow and expensive — what's the fallback if Meta rejects or throttles?
₹499/employee/month is aggressive; BambooHR charges $6-9/employee. Validate willingness to pay before building.
Attrition ML model needs 12+ months of data per company to be meaningful — cold start problem?
skeptic_flags: HRMS is a crowded market with deep-pocketed incumbents (Darwinbox, Keka, ZohoHR). Differentiation beyond WhatsApp is thin.
Payroll compliance in India changes every budget cycle — maintaining tax logic is a hidden ops burden.
Onboarding 'AI wizard' sounds like a feature, not a product. Why would companies switch entire HRMS for this?

pm.updated_mvp: Narrow to Leave + Payroll only for MVP. Drop attrition ML to v2. WhatsApp bot is a notification layer, not primary UI — build web app first.
pm.revised_differentiation: Position as 'compliance-first HRMS': auto-updates for India Budget changes, built-in auditor reports. Partner with 3 CA firms for co-marketing.
pm.de_risked_timeline: 3 months to paid pilot with 5 SMBs (not 6-month full build)
